sections.Goal: Build an automated content pipeline that generates, publishes, and monetizes [CONTENT_TYPE] for [TARGET_AUDIENCE] across [PLATFORMS]. The system handles research, creation, publishing, and revenue optimization autonomously.
sections.Why: Content scales with audience, not effort. AI can produce content at a pace impossible for humans — hundreds of articles, videos, or newsletters per month. Revenue compounds as content libraries grow and attract organic traffic over time.
sections.What: An automated content system with: AI-driven topic research and trending analysis, content generation pipeline (text/video/audio), multi-platform publishing automation, monetization integration (ads, affiliate, sponsorship), and performance analytics with feedback loops.
sections.Context: Tech stack: Next.js 15 (App Router), YouTube API for video publishing, OpenAI for content generation, Vercel for hosting. Content quality is critical — follow Golden Rule #2 (Zero-Waste Mindset) to validate every piece before publishing. Use Progressive Autonomy: start with human review of all content, then gradually automate approval for content types with consistent quality scores.
sections.Blueprint: Phase 1: Content research engine — identify trending topics and keywords in the niche. Phase 2: Generation pipeline — AI creates content drafts with quality scoring. Phase 3: Publishing automation — schedule and distribute to target platforms. Phase 4: Monetization setup — integrate ad networks, affiliate links, sponsorship management. Phase 5: Analytics and feedback loop — track performance and adjust generation parameters.
sections.Validation: Level 1: TypeScript compiles, ESLint passes. Level 2: Unit tests for content generation and publishing logic. Level 3: Integration test — generate content, verify quality score, publish to staging. Level 4: Platform API integration test — verify content appears correctly on each platform. Level 5: Revenue tracking test — verify monetization links and analytics are working.

validationGates.name: Syntax & Types
validationGates.description: Ensure code compiles and passes linting

validationGates.name: Unit Tests
validationGates.description: Run all unit tests with coverage reporting

validationGates.name: Content Quality Check
validationGates.description: Generate sample content and verify it passes quality thresholds

validationGates.name: Platform Publishing Test
validationGates.description: End-to-end test of content publishing to staging environments

commonPitfalls: Publishing AI content without quality review — always score and threshold-check before publishing
Ignoring platform-specific content guidelines — each platform has rules that can get accounts banned
Not diversifying across platforms — a single platform ban can wipe out the entire business
Generating duplicate or near-duplicate content — search engines penalize thin and duplicate content
Missing attribution for sourced information — always cite sources to maintain credibility
Over-optimizing for SEO at the expense of readability — content must serve the audience first
